entry.title: Unlock the Power of Intelligent Document Processing with SAP Document AI and Generative AI
entry.content:
Introduction

In today's fast-paced enterprise world, documents are everywhere — invoices, purchase orders, delivery notes, contracts, and more. Processing them manually is time-consuming, error-prone, and simply not scalable. That's where SAP Document AI comes in.

SAP Document AI (formerly known as Document Information Extraction) is a service on the SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP) that uses machine learning and, more recently, Large Language Models (LLMs) to automatically extract structured information from business documents. With the integration of Generative AI, it has become more powerful than ever — capable of handling not just standard document types, but virtually any custom document you throw at it.

In this blog post, I'll walk you through how SAP Document AI works, how to leverage Generative AI for document extraction, and why this matters for your SAP landscape.

What Is SAP Document AI?

SAP Document AI is an intelligent document processing service that enables businesses to:

  • Automatically extract key fields from documents (header fields, line items, etc.)
  • Support standard document types like invoices, payment advices, and purchase orders out of the box
  • Handle custom document types (delivery notes, birth certificates, contracts, etc.) using schema-based extraction
  • Integrate seamlessly with SAP S/4HANA, SAP Ariba, and other SAP solutions via API

At its core, the service uses pretrained machine learning models that are globally trained on vast datasets of business documents. But the real game-changer? The addition of Generative AI.

Generative AI + SAP Document AI: A Powerful Combination

Why Generative AI?

Traditional ML models are great for standard document types where the structure is well-known. But what about non-standard documents that vary in layout, language, or structure? This is where LLMs shine.

By combining SAP Document AI with Generative AI (LLMs), you can:

  • Define custom schemas for any document type without training a dedicated ML model
  • Use natural language descriptions as prompts to guide the LLM on what to extract
  • Get accurate extraction results even for documents with varying formats
  • Reduce the time-to-value dramatically — no lengthy ML training cycles required

Two Extraction Approaches in One Service

SAP Document AI now supports a hybrid approach:

Setup Type

Method

Best For

auto with Default Extractor

Pretrained ML Model

Standard fields in invoices, POs, payment advices

auto without Default Extractor

Generative AI (LLM)

Custom fields, non-standard documents

manual

Template-based

Highly structured, fixed-layout documents

This flexibility means you can mix and match extraction methods within the same schema, getting the best of both worlds.

How It Works: Step-by-Step

Here's a simplified walkthrough of how to use SAP Document AI with Generative AI:

Step 1: Set Up Your Environment

Start with an SAP BTP Trial or Free Tier account. Use the SAP BTP Booster to automatically provision the SAP Document AI service instance and subscribe to the Basic UI application. This sets up everything you need to get started quickly.

Step 2: Create a Schema

A schema defines the structure of the data you want to extract. Think of it as a template that tells the AI what information to look for.

  • Navigate to Schema Configuration in the SAP Document AI Basic UI

 

  • Click Create and give your schema a name (e.g., delivery_note_schema)

 

  • Select the appropriate Document Type — choose Custom for non-standard documents, or a specific type like Invoice for standard documents

Step 3: Define Your Fields

This is where Generative AI truly shines. For each field you want to extract:

 

  • Enter a field name (e.g., vendor_name, total_amount, delivery_date)
  • Choose a data type (string, number, date, currency, etc.)
  • Add a description — this is used as a prompt for the LLM

💡 Pro Tip: The description field is your prompt to the LLM. Write it as if you're explaining what you want to extract to a person with no prior context. The more specific you are, the better the extraction results!

For example:

  • Field: invoice_number
  • Description: "The unique identifier assigned to this invoice by the issuing company, typically found in the header area of the document."

Step 4: Activate the Schema

Once all fields are added, Activate the schema to move it from DRAFT to ACTIVE status. Only active schemas can be used for document extraction.

 

Step 5: Upload Documents and Extract

  • Navigate to the Document section in the UI

 

  • Upload your document (PDF, image, etc.)
  • Select your schema
  • The service processes the document and returns extracted field values in seconds

 

Real-World Use Cases

The combination of SAP Document AI and Generative AI opens up exciting possibilities:

  1. Accounts Payable Automation Automatically extract invoice details (vendor, amount, tax, line items) and post directly to SAP S/4HANA — reducing manual data entry and processing time.
  2. Logistics & Supply Chain Process delivery notes and shipping documents of any format, extracting quantities, dates, and goods information without custom ML training.
  3. HR & Compliance Extract information from onboarding documents, certificates, or contracts — even in multiple languages and varying formats.
  4. Cross-Industry Document Processing From healthcare (medical reports) to legal (contracts) to finance (bank statements) — if it's a document, SAP Document AI can handle it.

Integration with SAP Ecosystem

SAP Document AI isn't a standalone tool — it's designed to integrate deeply into the SAP ecosystem:

  • REST API: Integrate with any application using the standard SAP Document AI REST API via Swagger UI
  • SAP BTP CAP Applications: Build custom CAP-based applications that consume the Document AI service for automated document workflows
  • SAP Build: Use SAP Build Code or SAP Build Apps to create no-code/low-code automations powered by Document AI
  • SAP S/4HANA & Ariba: Direct integration for invoice processing and procurement document handling

Getting Started: Try It for Free

The best part? You can try SAP Document AI today at no cost using the SAP BTP Trial or Free Tier:

  1. Sign up for SAP BTP Trial
  2. Use the SAP BTP Booster to set up SAP Document AI
  3. Follow the hands-on tutorial on SAP Developers: Extract Information from Custom Documents with Generative AI

The trial provides a sandbox environment where you can experiment with both standard and custom document types using real sample files.

Key Takeaways

  • SAP Document AI combines machine learning and Generative AI (LLMs) for flexible, accurate document extraction
  • Custom schemas allow you to extract information from virtually any document type — no ML training required
  • Field descriptions act as LLM prompts — writing good descriptions directly improves extraction accuracy
  • The service is available on SAP BTP Trial and Free Tier, making it easy to experiment and build proof-of-concepts
  • Deep integration with the SAP ecosystem enables seamless end-to-end document automation

Conclusion

SAP Document AI with Generative AI is a transformative capability for any organization dealing with high volumes of documents. By removing the need for custom ML training while still delivering accurate structured extraction, it dramatically lowers the barrier to intelligent document processing.

Whether you're looking to automate accounts payable, streamline logistics, or build a custom document processing workflow — SAP Document AI gives you the tools to do it efficiently on SAP BTP.

I encourage you to try the hands-on tutorial and explore what's possible. Have questions or want to share your experience? Drop a comment below — I'd love to hear how you're using SAP Document AI in your projects!

References:

  • SAP Developers Tutorial: Extract Information from Custom Documents with Generative AI and SAP Document AI
  • SAP Developers Tutorial: Extract Information from Standard Documents with Generative AI and SAP Document AI
  • SAP Document AI on SAP BTP: Free Tier Setup

entry.title: Enable the excel template and import excel to upload the stocklevel data in Backoffice -SAP Commerce
entry.content:
I want to enable the download excel template and import excel to upload the stocklevel data in backoffice - SAP commerce cloud.

I enabled the download excel template and import excel by making changes in the backoffice-config.xml. But the problem is the stocklevel has a relation with warehouse and the warehouse in turn has relation with vendor. In the downloaded excel file I donot see the warehouse attribute in the column dropdown and without warehouse attribute we cannot import the stock level excel.

Debugged the code and found that there are filters that check for unique attribute and if its unique then those attributes gets enabled in excel.

Tried to make unique modifier true for code attribute in warehouse itemtype in the items.xml, but it didnot work, tried using redeclare as well, no luck. Below is the error

core-customize/hybris/bin/platform/resources/ant/antmacros.xml:587: java.lang.IllegalArgumentException: cannot merge namespace ((examplecore)) into ((<merged>)) due to duplicate attribute 'Warehouse.code(java.lang.String):((examplecore))::YAttributeDescriptor[examplecore-items.xml:519(AttributeTagListener)][PROPERTY]' vs 'Warehouse.code(java.lang.String):((basecommerce))::YAttributeDescriptor[basecommerce-items.xml:941(AttributeTagListener)][PROPERTY]'

So did it via, the impex:


UPDATE RelationDescriptor; enclosingType(code)[unique = true]; qualifier[unique = true]; unique
                          ; StockLevel                          ; warehouse                   ; true

UPDATE AttributeDescriptor; enclosingType(code)[unique = true]; qualifier[unique = true]; unique
                          ; Warehouse                          ; code                   ; true

UPDATE RelationDescriptor; enclosingType(code)[unique = true]; qualifier[unique = true]; unique
                         ; Warehouse                          ; vendor                   ; true

UPDATE AttributeDescriptor; enclosingType(code)[unique = true]; qualifier[unique = true]; unique
                          ; Vendor                          ; code                   ; true

After making the above changes, I see the attributes in excel template as required below:

But now the problem is when the build is deployed with no migrate data and in rolling update mode, the impex will not be run and modifiers of attributes flushes back to the initial configuration of unique as false for all the above attribute and relation descriptor.

Can some one help me in how to solve this issue??

entry.title: Re: All thw DTPs are in inactive state post SPS upgrade
entry.content:
There is no standard BW process, that would inactivate DTP-s specifically during an upgrade process. The DTP-s were either already inactive prior to the upgrade or someone did something which has lead to the deactivation (impact handling).

Even a manual systemcopy can easily lead to inactive DTP-s if not all steps are performed properly. This should not happen when PCA is used.

Btw RSDG_TRFN_ACTIVATE can be used to activate TRFN-s + DTP-s.

entry.title: Re: Create New Catalog, Space, Page, Section and Role – S4HANA Private Cloud
entry.content:
I have some observations.

1) Why posting this blog twice under the same tag?

Create New Catalog, Space, Page, Section and Role ... - SAP Community

Create New Catalog, Space, Page, Section and Role ... - SAP Community

2) There is nothing special on handling Fiori authorizations in S/4HANA Cloud Private edition (which btw is not written "S4HANA Private Cloud" as the title suggests)  other than on-premise edition.

3) This step by step uses /UI2/FLPD_CUST which is obsolete for at least 3 years already. Some snapshots on this tutorial partially show the messages where it says that.

4) There is nothing in this blog which is not yet very well documented and posted before. 

5) References should be done using /UI2/FLPCM_CONF or /UI2/FLPCM_CUST and not from the designer.

6) The post links the old Fiori Apps Library

Old: https://fioriappslibrary.hana.ondemand.com/sap/fix/externalViewer/

New: https://pr.alm.me.sap.com/launchpad#FALApp-display

7) It does not make sense to create a Space, a page and a section for the very same thing: "GL Reports"

8 ) If someone follow this step by step the app would not work as there are authorization objects needed to display data in this app.

9) Creating a catalog for a single app is not wrong but if someone does this every time a new app is requested the authorization concept becomes a nightmare. There are cleaver ways of assinging apps to users which the blog does not even mention.

10) The post completely ignored other apps which might navigate to this app or other apps which are linked to it

11) The role was never assigned to the user so this step by step would not work 😉

12) The introduction uses the poor excuse that functional consultants don't have to depend on ABAP or BASIS to achieve overall objective. What objective? Accessing a Fiori app or running over IT process? How is that different than requesting acccess to a tcode which should and should not belong to FICO?

First, authorizations are not handled by ABAP or BASIS in decent organizations. Fiori and SAP authoriations are separate skills.

But as you want incentivate running over IT process, why not explaining that the app will only work once the necessary components have been installed by BASIS + the OData services have been activated by someone? The same thing goes for the launchpad and the Manage Launchpad Spaces and Pages apps. They will only work if someone else has set them up.

As you are a single person persuing your objective while others are blocking you, why not creating the profile on your own? Why not assiging roles to your fellows functional consultants? Why not exposing this app to WorkZone in BTP if it is used as a central launchpad?

13) In decent IT landscapes and process, if you find an app which you should have access to, you request it by a ticket or something for people who can do the job for you. What you don't do is to try to grant the access yourself.

Even if some ABAP developer (with decent Fiori skills) is maintaining catalogs and spaces, this should not give them rights to decide how authorizations should be organized. Nor a functional consultant without decent Fiori skills

entry.title: Re: ACDOCA_M_EXTRACT vs MBEW
entry.content: For a stock report with per-unit price, you should not take the price directly from ACDOCA_M_EXTRACT. Instead, derive it from MBEW + relevant valuation tables, and then multiply with quantities from the stock tables.

entry.title: Troubleshooting Email Output in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition (2) – Four Frequent Errors
entry.content:
Overview

When setting up email output in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, several different errors can prevent emails from being sent. Unlike the scenario where emails appear as “Sent” but never arrive in the receiver’s inbox (an SPF/DKIM delivery issue, see the other blog Troubleshooting Email Output in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition (1) - Email not Reaching Recipient), the errors covered in this guide occur earlier in the process – the system refuses to even attempt sending the email because of missing or misconfigured settings.

This guide covers four common errors:

  • "No email recipient has been maintained"
  • "Recipient address x.y@abc.com not allowed"
  • "Sender domain in xxxx@xxxx.com is not allowed" / "Domain in sender email address is not allowed"
  • "Sender email address can't be initial"

Error 1: "No Email Recipient Has Been Maintained"

This error means the system could not find a recipient email address to send the output to. It affects the corresponding document types that use the EMAIL channel. The “To” field of the output item is empty.

The system looks for the recipient email address in two places, in order of priority:

  • First: A specific email address defined directly in the determination step “Email Recipient” of the Output Parameter Determination (OPD) app.
  • Second: If no DEFAULT email is maintained in OPD, the system reads the email address from the relevant business partner master data.

1.1 Fix in Output Parameter Determination (OPD)

  1. Open the Output Parameter Determination app and check the determination step “Email Recipient” for the relevant document type.
  2. Enter a specific recipient email address directly in the rule.

This takes the highest priority and overrides any address in the business partner master data.

1.2 Fix in Business Partner Master Data

If no default email is maintained in OPD, the email address must be maintained in the appropriate business partner master record. Where the email is stored depends on who the recipient is:

1.2.1 Recipient is a Customer (Taking Billing Document as an example):

  1. Open the Maintain Business Partner app.
  2. Search for the relevant customer business partner.
  3. Go to the “Address” tab.
  4. Ensure an email address is entered in the E-Mail field.

1.2.2 Recipient is a Contact Person (Taking Billing Document as an example):

  1. Open the Maintain Business Partner app and search with the customer number.
  2. Click the Relationships button.
  3. Find the relevant contact person and verify the Valid To date is set to 12/31/9999.
  4. Click the Change button.
  5. Under Address Data, click Assign Company Address.
  6. Enter the email address in the E-Mail field.

Note: If the contact person's Valid To date has expired, the system will not read the email address from that contact person record, even if an email is maintained there.

1.2.3 Recipient is a Supplier (Taking Payment Advice Document as an example):

For payment advice output processed through the Manage Automatic Payments app, the recipient email address is stored differently from customer outputs:

  1. Open the Maintain Business Partner app.
  2. Select the relevant business partner and the role “Supplier (Financial Accounting)”.
  3. Choose Company Code on the ribbon.
  4. Open the Vendor: Correspondence tab.
  5. Enter the recipient email address in the field Clrk's Internet add (Internet address of partner company clerk).

Error 2: "Recipient Address x.y@abc.com Not Allowed"

This error occurs in test systems only. SAP S/4HANA Cloud test systems have a built-in safeguard that restricts which recipient email domains the system is allowed to send emails to. This prevents test systems from accidentally sending emails to real external recipients.

If a recipient email address is correctly configured but the domain is not on the allowed list for that test system, the output will fail with this error.

2.1 How to Configure Allowed Receiver Domains (Test Systems)

  1. Open the configure activity “Define Email Receiver Addresses for Test Systems (ID: 101312)
  2. Add the allowed receiver domain(s). Entries must start with an asterisk (*). For example:

*@company.com          (allows all addresses in the company.com domain)
*@partner.com          (allows all addresses in the partner.com domain)

Note: This setting applies to test systems only and is not required in production systems. In production, the system can send to any valid email address without this restriction.

Error 3: "Sender Domain in xxxx@xxxx.com Is Not Allowed" / "Domain in Sender Email Address Is Not Allowed"

This error occurs when the sender email address used in Output Parameter Determination contains a domain that has not been registered as an allowed sender domain in the system configuration. This is a security control to prevent SAP from sending emails from domains the customer does not own or has not authorised.

This error can appear in two scenarios:

  • At output runtime: when an output item is processed and the sender domain has not been configured.
  • At OPD configuration time: when a user tries to define a Sender Email in Output Parameter Determination and the domain of that address is not registered.

3.1 How to Configure Allowed Sender Domains

  1. Open the configuration activity “Define Email Sender Domain (ID: 101313)”.
  2. Enter the domain(s) that are allowed as sender domains. The following formats are supported:

Sophia.Tester@company.com (allows emails sent to this email address)
*@company.com (allows all addresses with the domain company.com)
*.company.com (allows all email addresses in the subdomain company.com, e.g. Sophia@sales.company.com)

Warning: Do NOT include the @ character when entering a domain. For example, enter company.com, not @company.com. Including @ will cause the configuration to fail silently.

Warning: The SAP domain @sap.com must NOT be used in customer systems. Since SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2108, checks were introduced specifically to block the usage of SAP domains (sap.com or @sap.com) in the sender domain configuration.

Note: You can add multiple domains if your organisation sends from more than one domain (e.g., different domains per company code or region).

3.2 Also Define a Default Sender Email Address

As a best practice, also configure a default sender email address. This is used whenever no specific sender address is determined by the output logic. To configure it:

  1. Open the configuration activity “Define a Default Email Sender Address (ID: 101311)”.
  2. Enter a valid sender address from your registered domain, for example:

do.not.reply@company.com

Error 4: "Sender Email Address Can't Be Initial"

This error appears when the system cannot find any sender email address for the output. It is most commonly seen in payment advice processing through the Manage Automatic Payments app (App ID: F0770), but can also occur in other document types.

The error means that the Sender Email field in Output Parameter Determination for the relevant output type is either blank or has not been configured at all.

4.1 Fix: Maintain Sender Email in OPD for Payment Advice

  1. Open the Output Parameter Determination app.
  2. Set Show Rules for to, e.g.: Payment Advice.
  3. Set Determination Step to, e.g.: Email Settings.
  4. Choose the Edit button.
  5. Under Table Contents, choose + (Insert New Row) and fill in the following, e.g.:

Output type:      PAYM_ADV
Sender Email:     <your sender email address>
Email Template:   FFO_PAYM_ADVICE_EMAIL_TEMPLATE

  1. Save the configuration.

Note: The email template FFO_PAYM_ADVICE_EMAIL_TEMPLATE is delivered by SAP as a standard template. You can copy and customize it to adapt the subject line and email body to your business requirements.

Tip: Make sure the sender email domain is also registered in the allowed sender domains. Using an email address whose domain is not registered will result in the 'sender domain not allowed' error even after you fill in the sender email.

Quick Reference: Error Summary and Where to Fix

Error Message

Root Cause

Where to Fix

No email recipient has been maintained

Recipient email not configured in OPD or business partner master data

OPD (Email Recipient step) and/or Maintain Business Partner

Recipient address x.y@abc.com not allowed

Receiver domain not whitelisted on test/quality system

Config Activity ID 101312

Sender domain in xxxx@xxxx.com isn't allowed / Domain in sender email address is not allowed

Sender domain not registered as an allowed domain

Config Activity ID 101313 & 101311

Sender email address can't be initial

Sender email not configured in OPD for the output type

OPD > Show Rules for: &1 > Determination Step: Email Settings > Output type

entry.title: The Quiet Revolution in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud Logistics: Clean Core, Transparency, and AI
entry.content:
Supply chains are no longer just about moving physical goods; they are highly synchronized, data-driven ecosystems. As we navigate the latest updates in SAP S/4HANA—particularly with the "progress without disruption" philosophy of the 2025 releases—it is clear that the focus has shifted. We are moving away from reactive workarounds and toward proactive, intelligent, and strictly governed logistics.

For those of us working deep in the trenches of Sourcing, Procurement, and Warehouse Management, this shift brings both exciting capabilities and new operational challenges. Here is a look at the three pillars redefining our day-to-day logistics architecture in the Public Cloud.

1. The Discipline of Clean Core Extensibility

Historically, enforcing specific business rules—like making the "Requisitioner" or "Purchasing Organization" fields mandatory on a Purchase Requisition—often led to messy backend modifications. Today, the Public Cloud enforces a much-needed discipline.

Standard customizing has its limits, but leveraging Key User Extensibility is proving to be a game-changer. By utilizing specific Custom Logic (BAdIs) for Field Control (MM_PUR_S4_PR_FLDCNTRL_SIMPLE) and Item Checks (MM_PUR_S4_PR_CHECK), we can enforce strict data quality right at the entry point of procurement. This approach ensures compliance without compromising the clean core, guaranteeing that our systems remain upgrade-safe and resilient.

2. The Push for Total Process Transparency

True efficiency requires total visibility, yet there are still standard gaps we need to address as a community. A perfect example is workflow simulation.

With release 2602, SAP introduced the highly anticipated ability to simulate workflows for Purchase Orders, allowing teams to validate conditions before documents are processed. However, this same transparency is critically missing for Purchase Requisitions. PRs are the genesis of the procurement lifecycle. Without simulation capabilities at the PR level, organizations are left relying on reactive troubleshooting and trial-and-error. Pushing for these types of intuitive enhancements (like supporting IR #365219) is vital for maintaining process consistency from end to end.

3. Smarter Warehousing and System Resilience

Execution is where strategy meets reality. In the Warehouse Management space, the transition to Public Cloud highlights the need for advanced, automated housekeeping. While traditional EWM robustly handles rearrangement and slotting, Public Cloud WM environments still require creative approaches or scheduled optimization jobs to handle routine bin cleanups and stock consolidation effectively.

As we look toward the future of smart logistics—where predictive maintenance data and real-time equipment health will directly trigger warehouse and procurement tasks—backend stability is non-negotiable. SAP’s recent moves, such as introducing Multi-Availability Zones for the BTP ABAP environment and offering scalable 0.5 ACU runtimes for agile development, provide the exact infrastructure needed to build these high-volume, highly resilient integrations.

Bridging the Gap with AI

Finally, the tools to build this future are evolving faster than ever. From leveraging AI-powered UI5 plugins for rapid application development to integrating Joule into the end-user experience, the technology is ready. The remaining barrier is no longer software; it is the skills gap. As an ecosystem, our next major hurdle is moving past the hype and focusing on the tactical, day-one skills required to actually deploy these AI tools in live logistics environments.

What are your thoughts? Are you finding that Clean Core extensibility is fully meeting your complex procurement needs, or are there still gaps in standard Public Cloud WM functionality that your teams are struggling to work around? Let’s discuss in the comments below!

entry.title: Vibe Coding : Unlocking the Power of AI‑Assisted Development with VS Code, Cline, and SAP AI Core
entry.content:
AI‑assisted development is transforming how developers build, test, and optimize applications. Gone are the days of switching endlessly between documentation, terminals, and dashboards. Today, intelligent coding tools like Cline, integrated directly into VS Code, work alongside developers — understanding context, planning steps, and even writing code interactively.
When connected with SAP AI Core, this experience is elevated to an enterprise‑grade level: you can harness SAP’s generative AI capabilities right inside your Node.js projects without leaving your IDE. In this blog, we’ll walk through a complete setup and workflow to unleash this power step by step.   
 
🧩 Step 1: Set Up Your Prerequisites
Before we dive in, make sure you have:
  • VS Code (latest version)
  • Node.js 18+ installed
  • SAP BTP account with AI Core access (extended plan recommended)
  • Cline extension for VS Code
🧩 Step 2 : Installing Cline
  • Open VS Code.
  • Go to the Extensions tab (Ctrl+Shift+X or Cmd+Shift+X).
  • Search for “Cline”.
  • Install the official extension (no additional fork needed).
  • Restart VS Code.

 
🧩 Step 3 : Connect Cline to SAP AI Core
SAP AI Core gives Cline access to enterprise‑grade generative models in your SAP BTP environment. Integration is simple.
Get Your Service Key
  1. In your SAP BTP Cockpit, go to your subaccount.
  2. Under Services → Service Marketplace, find SAP AI Core.
  3. Create an instance (plan: extended).
  4. Generate a Service Key and copy the following information:
    • Client ID
    • Client Secret
    • Auth URL
    • Base URL
    • Resource Group (usually "default")
Please refer the steps as mentioned https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/ai-core-genaihub-provisioning..html=
Step 4: Configure Cline In VS Code

 

In VS Code, open Cline → Settings → API Configuration, and fill in:

 
Cline Setting SAP AI Core Value
AI Core Client Idclientid
AI Core Client Secretclientsecret
AI Core Base URLserviceurls/AI_API_URL
AI Core Auth URLauth_url
AI Core Resource Group"default"

Once saved, Cline will connect securely to your SAP AI Core environment. You're now running on SAP’s Generative AI Hub infrastructure.

 

Step 5: Verify That Cline Is Working
  1. Click the Cline icon in the VS Code sidebar
  2. Type a test prompt, such as: “Hi”
  3. Press Enter
💡Step 6: Start a Node.js Project with AI

Now it’s time to let Cline assist you from the beginning.

 Project Setup Instruction
## Step 1: Create a  project 
Create an empty Nodejs CAP project 
this creates 
 

 

## Step 2: Environment Setup
Create a environment  file and set the port to 4000
## Step 3: Express Server Setup
Create a file to load the env variables, and start express on that port. Add a simple /health route and add a GET /employees route that uses axios to fetch users from "https://jsonplaceholder.typicode.com/users" and return only their name and email

## Step 4: CAP Data Model

 

Create a entity called Employees that has ID as Integer, name as String, and email as String
## Step 5: CAP Service Definition
Create a service to expose the Employees entity as a projection, and add an unbound action called fetchTodos that takes an employeeId Integer and returns a String array
this creates
## Step 6: Override Employees READ Operation

 

Override the READ of Employees. Do NOT use a database. Instead, use axios to GET https://jsonplaceholder.typicode.com/users, map the results to match the CDS entity fields, and return the array
 ## Step 7: Implement fetchTodos Action

 

Implement the fetchTodos action in the same file. Use axios to get todos from https://jsonplaceholder.typicode.com/users/:employeeId/todos and return an array of just the todo titles

## Step 8: Run Locally

You can either use Cline to run and scaffold the project, or directly execute the standard CAP command like cds watch to start the application and test the services.

🎯Conclusion

Combining VS Code, Cline, and SAP AI Core brings a new era of Node.js development — one where natural language drives coding, SAP‑grade AI enriches solutions, and productivity soars. From scaffolding APIs to integrating intelligent services, this workflow empowers developers to focus on creativity instead of configuration.

The fusion of agentic coding and SAP’s enterprise AI stack isn’t just productivity‑boosting — it’s redefining how SAP developers build the future.

Ready to start vibing?
💬Install Cline, connect to your SAP AI Core, and experience AI‑first Node.js development today.

entry.title: Re: The application 'Test your Processes' is not running.
entry.content:
Dear @Ryo_Yokoyama,

In Public Cloud this issue is usually caused by the Test Automation Tool setup, especially the DEFAULT user / role credentials rather than by the J58 process itself. SAP documents this exact symptom test plans in Test Your Processes can remain at 0%, not progress or stay in In Process status. SAP recommended checks are to validate the test user credentials, check the authentication/IDP setup, stop the stuck execution and then re-execute the test plan

More details below:

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3087418

The key checks are:

1. In Test Your Processes, open Maintain Roles and verify the user maintained for DEFAULT

2. Confirm that the DEFAULT user exists in the S/4HANA Cloud system is not locked or expired and has valid credentials

3. Log in manually with the same test user credentials to confirm that the password is valid

4. Re-maintain the DEFAULT credentials in Maintain Roles

More details below:

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3082337

5. Check that all test users/roles used by the process steps are valid and not locked, expired or maintained with an incorrect password. SAP confirms that issues with business users, roles or DEFAULT setup can stop the test plan execution from progressing

More details below:

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3557201

6. If SSO or an external Identity Provider is used, verify the login setup. SAP specifically mentions SSO / unsupported external IDP as a possible reason for the 0% issue in some cases the resolution is to use valid credentials instead of SSO for the test execution

More details below:

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/2820953

7. Stop the stuck execution and run the test plan again after correcting the setup. SAP also lists insufficient execution resources as a possible cause and recommends stopping the ongoing execution and re-executing the test plan

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: Automatic batch determination with releasing the production order
entry.content:
Hi all,

I am working on S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition and trying to set up automatic batch determination for components when a production order is released.

In the material master (MRP 2 view), the field 'Batch Entry' only offers value 3 – 'Batch Determination at Order Release', but not value 4, which  triggers the batch determination fully automatically in the background during order release.

With value 3, it seems the system only checks whether a batch determination is expected, but does not execute it automatically – meaning the user still has to manually click the 'Batch Determination' button in the component list of the production order.

My questions:
1. Is value 4 intentionally not available in S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition (Clean Core / reduced scope)?
2. Is there any standard way to trigger batch determination fully automatically upon production order release in the Public Cloud, without manual user interaction?
3. Or is the recommended SAP Best Practice in the Public Cloud to perform batch determination at the time of goods issue rather than at order release?

Any experience or guidance from the community would be greatly appreciated!

Thanks in advance.

Jana

entry.title: SAP Inside Track Sydney 2026 | Friday September 4, 9:00am - 6pm
entry.content:
SAP Inside Track Sydney 2026

A reimagined SAP community event focused on real-world insights, collaboration, and learning.


Event Details

Date: Friday, September 4, 2026 | 9am to 6pm
Location: The Mint, 10 Macquarie Street, Sydney
Format: Full-day, in-person event


Registration

Registration information will be published soon.

Attention: Registration for the event will take place using a separate event platform and not via the attendance RSVP section on the right-hand side.

Please follow this page for updates on attendee registration, agenda announcements, speaker confirmations, and other event news.

About the Event

SAP Inside Track Sydney 2026 brings the SAP Community together for a full day of learning, connection, and practical insight.

This year’s event is a refreshed take on the traditional SAP Inside Track format, with an elevated venue experience, a sharper agenda, and a strong focus on content that is relevant to today’s business environment and SAP landscape.

Expect a day of presentations, demos, hands-on labs, shaped by knowledge sharing, community spirit, and relevant discussion across the key topics influencing SAP customers and practitioners today.

Whether you are interested in enterprise architecture, SAP strategy, development, automation, data and AI, or customer experience, SAP Inside Track Sydney 2026 is designed to create valuable opportunities to learn from others, exchange ideas, and strengthen connections across the local SAP ecosystem.


Why Attend

  • Hear practical perspectives from SAP customers, partners, and community speakers
  • Explore key topics across architecture, development, automation, data and AI, strategy, and product updates
  • Connect with peers from across the SAP ecosystem
  • Experience a refreshed SAP Inside Track in a standout Sydney CBD venue


Venue

SAP Inside Track Sydney 2026 will be held at The Mint, one of Sydney’s most historic and distinctive venues.

Its heritage character, combined with a striking modern auditorium and courtyard surrounds, creates a unique backdrop for a reimagined SAP community event.

Located on Macquarie Street in the Sydney CBD, The Mint is conveniently within walking distance of Martin Place, Town Hall, and Wynyard.

The Mint, Sydney


Content Themes and Agenda

The agenda will feature a mix of presentations and demos designed to combine strategic insight with practical, real-world experience.

Planned content themes include:

  • Enterprise Architecture
  • Customer Stories
  • Development
  • Automation
  • Data and AI
  • SAP Strategy
  • Product Roadmaps and Updates

A draft agenda format is provided below.  More details on the agenda will be shared as the event planning progresses. 
Additionally, there will be breakout streams for hands-on labs and demos.

TimeSession
09:00am - 09:25amRegistration

04:50pm - 05:00pmWrap-up and SAP Press book draw
05:00pm - 06:00pmNetworking Drinks

 

Call for Speakers

Details on the call for speakers process will be announced soon.

If you have a customer story, technical deep dive, architecture perspective, or lessons learned to share, we would love to hear from you.

Sponsorship

SAP Inside Track Sydney 2026 is supported by sponsors who help make this elevated community experience possible.

Sponsorship opportunities will be shared shortly.   We look forward to partnering with organisations that want to support the SAP Community and contribute to a high-quality, community-led event experience.

A Community-Led Event

SAP Inside Track events are created by the SAP Community, for the SAP Community.

SAP Inside Track Sydney 2026 aims to build on that spirit while introducing a refreshed event experience that combines community energy with a more polished venue, strong content, and meaningful opportunities for networking and collaboration.

Stay Updated

entry.title: Re: Email notification to purhaser on award of quotation...
entry.content:
Dear @Talha_Naeem-071,

No, this is not available as a standard Public Cloud configuration without workflow. If Flexible Workflow is not to be used Public Cloud does not provide a standard setting that sends an email to the purchaser exactly when a supplier quotation is awarded against an RFQ

Possible approach without Flexible Workflow:

1. Use SAP BTP / SAP Integration Suite as the extension layer

2. Detect the award indirectly by using released integration content:

    • Supplier Quotation API/service to read supplier quotation data. SAP documents the service for reading, modifying, deleting and creating supplier quotations with reference to an RFQ

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/bb9f1469daf04bd894ab2167f8132a1a/72953820cc7c47fc9cdacd9702decf4d.html

    • RFQ business events where applicable. SAP documents RFQ events including an event raised when a follow-on document is created

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/bb9f1469daf04bd894ab2167f8132a1a/11992e4361e64e8b875d06705d73434c.html

3. Check whether the quotation / RFQ has reached the relevant awarded or follow-on-document-created state

4. Determine the purchaser from the RFQ / purchasing group / internal responsibility mapping

5. Send the email from the extension layer

6. Store a notification log so the same award does not trigger duplicate emails 

SAP supports event-based extensions in Public Cloud through Business Event Handling / Event Mesh but this requires the relevant event topic to be available and activated for the tenant. SAP documents that events from Public Cloud can be published and consumed by BTP applications and that Business Event Handling scope item 1NN plus SAP Event Mesh are part of this setup

More details below:

https://learning.sap.com/courses/implementing-sap-s-4hana-cloud-public-edition/setting-up-customer-driven-integrations

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0f69f8fb28ac4bf48d2b57b9637e81fa/8cbf952e55364254be2da77aa1342aa5.html

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: 在SAP S/4HANA Cloud 公有云生产系统中更改会计年度变式
entry.content:
真实世界的业务是动态的。合并、收购、售卖公司或新的战略报表要求可能意味着您现有的会计年度不再满足变化的需要。

您可能会在以下情况中找到和您相同的处境：

* 场景 1：需要调整会计年度：贵公司一直在日历年（1 月 - 12 月）运营，但由于最近收购，您现在需要与会计年度从 7 月开始的新母公司保持一致。这需要创建“缩短的会计年度”，以弥合从 12 月到 7 月新会计年度开始的差距。

* 场景 2：重新定义期间：您将从标准日历月移动到零售日历，如 4-4-5 周结构。这从根本上改变了全年的过账期间定义方式。

如果您已经进入系统配置尝试进行更改，则可能会发现会计年度变式 (FYV) 字段在生产环境中不可编辑。我想解释为什么会这样，更重要的是，引导您找到正确的解决方案。

挑战：为什么我不能改动呢？

会计年度定义是财务系统中最基本的配置之一。基本上从统一日记账 (ACDOCA) 中的日记账分录到计划数据 (ACDOCP) 和资产价值的每个财务交易都与会计年度和过账期间相关联。

在没有结构化方法的情况下，在生产系统中回溯更改此定义，就像在您仍然生活在房屋中时尝试更改房屋的承重结构。这可能会导致严重的数据不一致和错误报告。由于此复杂性和跨不同应用范围的深层相关性，因此在生产系统中不允许此更改。

解决方案：由专家主导的专门项目

对于 SAP S/4HANA Cloud（公共版），生产系统中会计年度定义的更改将作为 SAP Data Management and Landscape Transformation (DMLT) 咨询服务提供的客户个人咨询项目进行处理。

这不仅仅是一个简单的配置切换；它是一种全面的服务，包括：

* 分析：专家将与您一起了解所需变更的全部范围和影响。

* 配置：将仔细配置新的会计年度变式。

* 数据转换：如有必要，将转换主数据和事务数据以与新结构保持一致。

* 支持：该团队将为您提供客户特定步骤的支持，以确保所有相关任务和应用程序的平稳过渡。

我们强烈建议您提前对此进行计划。在流程早期与审计员互动，并为项目留出足够的提前期。

如何开始

准备好采取下一步行动了吗？以下是官方流程：

1.发起请求：要正式请求更改会计年度定义，请使用组件 CA-LT-SRV-2CL 创建案例。

2.早期参与：如前所述，您联系 SAP DMLT 咨询服务越早，我们为您准备和计划服务就越好。

需要牢记的重要注意事项

为确保您清楚了解，以下是有关服务的几个要点：

* 系统前提条件：此服务支持通过 SAP Central Business Configuration (CBC) 管理的 SAP S/4HANA Cloud（公共版）实施。

* 范围：目前，该服务涵盖跨所有分类账和公司代码分配的单个唯一会计年度变式的变更。

* 新变式标识：如果您当前正在使用预配置的 SAP 会计年度变式（例如 K4），则项目将需要创建新的客户特定变式标识（例如 Z4）。作为服务的一部分，包含旧变式标识（如表 ACDOCA 中的行项目）的数据将转换为新变式标识。

* 面向未来的变更：对会计年度定义的更改仅影响未来的数据和流程。历史交易数据的修改不在范围内。

*进一步参考 3678328 - Change of Fiscal Year Variant in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

您的财务框架至关重要，应精准、谨慎地根据不断变化的业务需求调整财务框架。虽然您无法通过单击开关简单的进行此更改，但可以使用结构化、专家主导的路径来确保其正确完成。

entry.title: Re: Howdo we handle Q stock posting through QM management...
entry.content:
Thank you for your query.

Based on my understanding, QM is active for the material and the stock is posted to Quality Inspection stock (Q‑stock), while there is a requirement to move the stock before a Usage Decision (UD) is made.

In SAP standard, if no UD has been taken, the only possible way to move Q‑stock is via T-code QAC2 (Stock Posting for Inspection Lot). These postings remain fully under QM governance and audit control.

To the best of my knowledge, SAP S/4HANA does not provide a standard “rolling” or partially released Q‑stock concept that allows unrestricted or standard logistics movements while keeping the stock under QM control. This would contradict the core QM design principle, where Q‑stock represents a strong logistical and quality lock. In this respect, the behavior of Quality Inspection stock in S/4HANA standard QM is the same as in ECC.

entry.title: Re: How to capture all the existing Fiori catalogs and ti...
entry.content:
Hello,

Please consider the following steps:

Capturing Fiori Catalogs & Tiles in a Single TR
Step 1: Create a TR
SE09 → Create → Workbench/Customizing Request
Note down TR number (e.g., DEV#K900123)

Step 2: Mass Transport of All Catalogs & Tiles
SE38 → Run Program: /UI2/FLPCM_TRANSPORT_ALL
Select: Catalogs, Tiles, Groups/Spaces, Pages
Assign your TR number → Execute (F8)

Step 3: Transport Role-Based Tiles (PFCG)
PFCG → Open each Role → Menu Tab → Transport
Assign to the SAME TR number

Step 4: Verify TR Contents
SE09 → Open TR → Verify objects:
- FCLC = Catalog
- FCLT = Tile
- FGRP = Group
- AGR_HIERT = Role Menu

Step 5: Release & Transport
SE09 → Release Task → Release TR → STMS (Import to target)

Best regards,

Joe

entry.title: Error during Mass Maintenance: "Business Partner with GUID ... already exists"
entry.content:
Hi,

I am currently working on mass-updating Business Partners (BP) using the "Manage Mass Maintenance" app to set them to inactive status.

Context:

  1. I exported the current BP master data using "Export Master Data - Business Partners".

  2. In the file, I mapped the following columns: SOURCE_ID, PARTNER, XBLCK, NAME_ORG1, NAME_ORG2, NAME_ORG3, VALID_FROM, and VALID_TO.

  3. To deactivate the BPs, I set the XBLCK field to 'X' and uploaded the file back into the "Manage Mass Maintenance - Business Partner" process.

Issue:
Instead of updating the existing records, the system returns the following error for many BPs:

"Business Partner with GUID [GUID_VALUE] already exists"

It seems the system is trying to create a new BP record instead of performing an update/change on the existing one, despite the PARTNER number being included in the input file.

Questions:

  1. Why does the system treat an update request as an attempt to create a duplicate record with the same GUID?

  2. Is there a specific configuration or an additional field (e.g., action code or specific indicator) required to explicitly instruct the system to "Update" rather than "Create"?

  3. What are the mandatory columns and their specific values required to perform an "Inactive" status update for existing BPs in the "Manage Mass Maintenance" app? (For example, is XBLCK alone sufficient, or do I need to include specific validity dates or action indicators?)

Any guidance on the correct procedure for mass-deactivating existing BPs via this app would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you.

Hello 

You can either lock the contract for any further valuation or periodic posting by changing the contract status and setting a lock.

You have set both the locks master data and account assignment lock.

 

Or if you really want to delete the contract, you need to enable the an configuration for contract deletion.

RECACUST -> Tools  -> Deletion of Real Estate Objects Without Archiving
Click the check box on contract and you will be able to delete the contract in RECN window

 

Warm regards,

Veer

entry.title: Intercompany Logistics transactional Automation for Tax and Tariff Optimization
entry.content:
Executive Summary:

Global tax and tariff volatility, together with rapid inorganic growth of multinational companies, is increasing the complexity of modern supply chains. This is becoming more complex to determine the impact on Gross profitabilityAs Gross profitability=price of goods sold-(cost of goods manufactured+logistics cost)and is directly impacted by cross-border levies. Estimates suggest that taxes, tariffs, and duties can account for roughly 25% of potential gross profit erosion or contribution. By designing tariff-optimized supply chains.

Medical devices and life sciences products are subject to stringent regulations by the FDA, the European MDD/MDR, and other regional regulatory authorities. Additionally, significant taxation and tariff discrepancies arise when the product‑owning entity, manufacturing location, and target markets are based in different countries or continents.

Organizations can reduce total landed costs and tariff exposure by systematically identifying and operationalizing tax‑ and duty‑optimization opportunities across the end‑to‑end supply chain. This requires near‑real‑time visibility into tariff schedules, regulatory changes, and global trade rules, supported by digital tools that enable dynamic decision‑making. Practical use cases include lane reconfiguration, precise Harmonized Tariff Schedule (HTS) determination, and the application of duty‑mitigation instruments to reduce liabilities and reclaim eligible credits.

While ERP platforms such as SAP, JDE, and Oracle provide strong transactional foundations, they lack a fully integrated solution for automating supply‑chain execution optimized for tax and tariff outcomes. The industry requires an advanced platform that enables supply‑chain planners to model and configure physical material flow lanes, systematically associate those lanes with HTS codes and mitigation strategies. Automate execution of transactions through a virtual (digital) node. This digital node would orchestrate transactions and transmit compliant, enriched data to physical plants and warehouses, ensuring both operational efficiency and regulatory compliance.

Achieving these benefits typically requires reconfiguring transactional and logistical flows, reorganizing legal entities and the ownership of the product based HTS code while maintaining robust governance to ensure full compliance with global trade regulations.

This white paper defines a practical, ERP‑enabled framework for designing legally compliant, tariff‑optimized supply chains by combining:

 (a) Lane and entity redesign,

 (b) A digital orchestration layer (virtual node) that automates intercompany transactional flows,

 (c) Robust governance and compliance controls.

 

Background and Problem Statement

  • Modern supply chains span multiple legal entities, production sites, and markets. When product ownership, manufacturing location, and destination markets differ, tariff and tax treatment becomes complex and often unpredictable.
  • Gross profitability impact due to cross border levies (tariffs, duties, taxes) are embedded in logistics cost and can substantially change realized margins.
  • Existing ERP systems (SAP, Oracle, JDE) provide reliable transactional and inventory foundations but lack an integrated capability to:
    • Model and simulate tariff outcomes across alternative physical and legal flows.
    • Automate intermediate intercompany transactions (e.g., STOs, intercompany invoices, transfer postings) to reflect tariff‑optimized virtual structures.
    • Orchestrate enriched, compliant trade data to physical sites in near real time.

Real-world case studies demonstrate the scale of value at stake:

  • A Fortune 500 U.S. retailer avoided $3.2M in tariffs and achieved a 31% reduction in total landed cost by rerouting freight into Malaysian free‑trade zones, reclassifying HTS codes, and shifting part of its sourcing to Turkey during the 2025 China–U.S. tariff escalation.
  • A global high‑tech manufacturer, facing sudden U.S. tariff spikes, reconfigured its supply chain within days—including shifting production between Mexico and Europe and leveraging Free Trade Zones (FTZs)—to maintain full-service levels while reducing overall tariff exposure.
  • A consumer products manufacturer offset a 25% tariff on key materials by redesigning products, altering packaging, shifting outsourced production, and deploying enhanced market intelligence and competitive pricing strategies to margins.

These cases illustrate how agile supply chain network redesign, tariff engineering, FTZ utilization, and advanced regulatory‑tracking technologies can significantly reduce tariff exposure while improving speed and resilience.

Realizing these benefits demands strong governance and strict adherence to global trade compliance requirements. This white paper provides the key levers for designing an ERP‑enabled, legally compliant framework that supports the development of tariff‑efficient supply chains

This framework enables organizations to:

  1. Tariff optimization,
  2. Evaluates associated compliance risks,
  3. Rapidly implement systematic supply‑lane changes in response to tax and tariff changes without changing physical movements of goods.
  4. Automate virtual systematic transactions flows

Systematic Solution Design Framework

This is time sensitive and so we must have a comprehensive solution framework. This white paper describes the process for automating the intermediate STO processing through virtual facilities which would ultimately help reduce the manual efforts in posting further documents.

To address this exposure, organizations can design tariff‑optimized supply chains that reduce total landed cost and mitigate duty impact. Achieving these efficiencies requires not only structural redesign of physical and systematic transactional flows—including the adoption of virtualized transaction models—but also the deployment of advanced tools and technologies that provide real‑time visibility into tariff changes, global trade regulations, and HTS code–specific mitigation opportunities. With timely insights, companies can reconfigure supply chain lanes to lower duty burdens and capture available credits or refunds.

 

1.Key Drivers and Constraints

  • Drivers
    • Trade policy volatility is based on geopolitical dynamics (e.g., sudden tariffs, quota changes).
    • Cross‑border corporate restructuring and M&A activity increasing entity complexity.
    • The need to preserve margins in low‑margin industries (medical devices, life sciences, consumer, high tech).
  • Constraints
    • Regulatory complexity for regulated products (FDA, EU MDR/MDD, other regional authorities) compliance cannot be compromised.
    • Customs valuation, country‑of‑origin rules, HTS/HS classification accuracy, and record-keeping obligations.
    • Operational constraints: lead times, plant capacity, and service‑level requirements.

2.Regulatory Considerations (Medical Devices and Life Sciences)

  • Highly regulated product categories require:
    • Traceability of product changes, labeling, and manufacturing site information.
    • Strict change control when moving production or ownership to avoid nonconformities with regulatory filings.
    • Coordination between quality/regulatory affairs and trade/compliance teams before implementing lane or entity changes.
  • Tariff engineering strategies must be validated against product safety and regulatory registrations; any physical change to product composition or manufacturing process triggers regulatory assessment.

3.Principal Levers for Tariff Optimization

  • Network and Lane Engineering
    • Reroute flows through low‑duty jurisdictions or free trade zones (FTZs).
    • Shift production between locations to leverage preferential trade agreements and lower duty rates.
  • HTS/HS Code Optimization and Classification Hygiene
    • Precise HTS determination reduces misclassification risk and exposure to higher duties.
    • Continuous review of classification as product designs evolves.
  • Duty Mitigation Instruments
    • Utilize FTZs, inward processing relief, bonded warehouses, and drawback claims wherever eligible.
    • Apply tariff preference programs and free trade agreements with proper documentation.
  • Legal Entity and Ownership Reorganization
    • Align product ownership and invoicing entities to legitimately capture tariff advantages while maintaining transfer pricing and tax compliance.
  • Product and Packaging Redesign
  • Minor product or packaging changes that do not compromise regulatory compliance can alter classification and duty exposure.
  • Improves accuracy of customs filings and auditability.

Systematic Solution Design Framework (Two Part) Part A — Connected Visibility of Tariff Flows

  • Real‑time tariff and regulation feed integration (global tariff schedules, HTS updates, regulatory alerts).
  • Lane‑level costing engine that models landed cost including duties, taxes, freight, insurance, and handling.
  • Analytics and simulation for “what‑if” scenario planning (e.g., new tariff imposition, FTZ routing).
  • Role‑based dashboards for planners, trade compliance, and finance.

Part B — Legal Entity Reorganization & ERP Automation

  • Legal design aligning products, ownership, and invoicing lawful tariff outcomes.
  • ERP configuration to support automated intermediate transactions:
    • Virtual facility constructs (non‑physical storage/transaction nodes) in the system.
    • Automatic creation of STOs, intercompany invoices, and inventory postings tied to tariff strategy.
    • Integration points to customs filing platforms and warehouse management systems (WMS).
  • Controls and audit trails embedded in transaction flows.

Design Patterns for Transaction Flow

  • Common flow topologies (examples):
    • P → V → C (Physical location → Virtual node → Customer)
    • P → V → P → C (Physical → Virtual processing → Physical → Customer)
    • P → P → V → C (Multi‑physical moves consolidated into a virtual node → Customer)
  • Automation must ensure inventory visibility and prevent double counting or inventory imbalance.
  • Example: a virtual node can absorb a physical inbound and reissue an intercompany outbound, so customs treatment reflects the preferred ownership and valuation method.

Automation of Intermediate STO Processing Through Virtual Facilities (Figure 1,2 &3)

  • Goal: Reduce manual effort and posting errors by automating intermediate Stock Transport Orders (STOs) and reconciliations.
  • Functional components:
    • Rule engine to decide when to trigger virtual postings and what documents to generate.
    • Mapping between lane, HTS code, and posting rules (inventory valuation, duty assignment).
    • Exception handling workflows for reconciliation, disputed classification, and regulatory hold.
  • Outcome: faster book‑to‑customs alignment, fewer manual journal entries, and reduced lead time for customs filings.

 

Figure 1 Programming Process flow

 

 

Figure 2 Reverse Logistics Flow

 

Governance, Compliance, and Controls

  1. Governance pillars:
    • Cross‑functional operating model: Trade Compliance + Tax + Supply Chain + Regulatory Affairs + Finance + IT.
    • Policy framework: classification policy, valuation policy, entity ownership matrix, and FTZ/relief policy.
    • Change control: regulatory offs before any product/plant/packaging modification.
  2. Controls:
    • Automated validation of HTS-to-product mappings with periodic reconciliations.
    • Audit logs for virtual node transactions and customs‑facing data.
    • Periodic third‑party customs audits and internal compliance testing.
  3. Transfer Pricing and Tax Considerations:
    • Any entity and flow changes require transfer pricing assessment and documentation.
    • Coordination with tax counsel to avoid unintended tax consequences when reorganizing ownership or invoicing.

Implementation Roadmap (High Level)

  1. Assessment (0–3 months)
    • Current state mapping: lanes, entities, HTS profiles, relief instruments used, and ERP capabilities.
    • Opportunity sizing and risk assessment.
  2. Design (2–4 months)
    • Define target legal/entity models, virtual node architecture, and ERP posting rules.
    • Develop classification and mitigation playbook.
  3. Build & Pilot (3–6 months)
    • Implement tariff feeds, lane costing engine, and virtual node prototypes.
    • Pilot on low‑risk SKUs and one logistics lane or FTZ.
  4. KPIs and Value Metrics
  • Cost and margin
    • Reduction in total landed cost (%)
    • Duty savings ($) and % of margin preserved

 

Conclusion

  • Tariff and tax volatility will continue to be important drivers of supply chain competitiveness. Companies that adopt an ERP‑enabled, digitally orchestrated approach to intercompany transactional automation and lane optimization can materially reduce total landed cost while maintaining service and regulatory compliance.
  • The combination of legal entity alignment, virtual transaction models, automated STO/invoice processing, and continuous regulatory monitoring delivers scalable and auditable tariff efficiency.
  • Implemented responsibly, this approach preserves quality and regulatory obligations (critical in medical devices and life sciences) while unlocking near‑term and ongoing margin recovery opportunities.

entry.title: Re: Release a transport without releasing the task
entry.content:
Hello Linh,

From a Cloud ALM Feature we are triggering the release of the transport.

You can check at CTS-TMS level that if you have created a transport with a task:

- If the task is empty and you have content at transport level, even a comment at transport level you will be able to release the transport

- If the task is not empty it is not possible to release the transport

So, from Cloud ALM when you click on the Release link we trigger the release of the transport and CTS will do the rest according to the CTS logic explained above Nothing that Cloud ALM can interfere here.

Best regards,

Dolores

entry.title: PO Workflow Restart in Public Cloud – Options and Differences in Start Behaviour (Fiori vs GUI Apps)
entry.content:
In SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud, Purchase Orders can be approved using Flexible Workflow for Purchase Orders. This approval process ensures compliance and governance in procurement processes.

In real-life business scenarios, however, it is quite common that a Purchase Order must be changed or reworked after initial approval—for example, due to price changes, quantity adjustments, or updated delivery dates.

To handle such changes correctly, SAP provides several options to control when a workflow restart should be triggered. Nevertheless, during implementation and testing, differences in restart behavior can be observed depending on whether the Purchase Order is changed via a Fiori app (Manage Purchase Orders) or via the Web GUI (Create Purchase Order) (ME22N).

This blog post summarizes the available options to control workflow restarts, highlights an observed behavioral difference, and explains the underlying cause.

Restarting Approval Workflows for Purchase Orders

 

Standard Configuration: SSCUI 103345Enhancement via BAdI: MMPUR_PO_WORKFLOW_RESTART

SAP provides a standard configuration node to control workflow restarts:

Manage Conditions to Restart Flexible Workflow for Purchase Orders (SSCUI 103345)

Using this configuration, you can define pre-defined conditions that determine whether a workflow must be restarted when an already approved Purchase Order is changed. Typical examples include:

  • Net Price changes
  • Quantity changes
  • Incoterm changes

This approach works well as long as the required logic fits into the predefined conditions delivered by SAP.

When additional or customer-specific business conditions need to be evaluated, SAP offers a Business Add-In:

MMPUR_PO_WORKFLOW_RESTART

This BAdI allows you to implement your own logic to decide whether a workflow restart is required when a Purchase Order is changed.

Typical use cases include conditional restarts:

  • Restarting workflows only for specific document types
  • Considering custom fields or business rules
  • Excluding certain changes from triggering a restart

From a functional point of view, this BAdI provides the necessary flexibility to fine-tune workflow behavior.

 

Observed Difference in Restart Behavior

During implementation of BAdI MMPUR_PO_WORKFLOW_RESTART, an important difference can be observed depending on the application used to change the Purchase Order:

  • Fiori App: Manage Purchase Orders
  • Web GUI App: Change Purchase Order (ME22N)

Observation

When the same Purchase Order change is performed:

  • The workflow restart behavior differs between the Fiori app and ME22N
  • The custom logic implemented in the BAdI is not always evaluated in the same way
  • As a result, a workflow may restart in one app, but not in the other

This behavior can be confusing, especially during testing, as users expect consistent workflow handling regardless of the UI used.

Root Cause

The root cause of this behavior is explained in SAP KBA Note 3736482 - Workflow restart BADI MMPUR_PO_WORKFLOW_RESTART fiori app vs me22n - SAP for Me.

The workflow framework interprets certain changes differently depending on the UI technology

  • Different technical handling between the odata fiori and the ABAP core. 
  • When a PO change is made, at the BADI call, the 'purchaseorderitem' table is always filled with all items in a fiori call, but from ME22n the 'purchaseorderitem' table is only filled with the item being changed. 
    If a header only change is made, the 'purchaseorderitem' table will be empty in ME22n and it will contain all items from a fiori change.   

Key Insight

This is not a customizing error, but a system behavior difference described by SAP.

Summary

To summarise:

  • SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud offers standard configuration (SSCUI 103345) to control workflow restarts for Purchase Orders
  • For advanced logic, BAdI MMPUR_PO_WORKFLOW_RESTART can be used
  • When implementing this BAdI, different restart behavior may occur depending on whether changes are made via:
    • Fiori apps, or
    • Web GUI apps

When designing workflow restart logic, it is therefore important to:

  • Test scenarios using both Fiori and GUI apps
  • Be aware that identical business changes may not trigger workflows consistently across UIs

References

    • 3736482 - Workflow restart BADI MMPUR_PO_WORKFLOW_RESTART fiori app vs me22n - SAP for Me

entry.title: Re: Automatic batch determination with releasing the prod...
entry.content:
If your requirement is to validate or enforce batch determination for components at release, here are the practical options:

  • Keep Batch Entry = 3. Use business process controls or flexible workflow to prevent order release completion unless batches are assigned to all components. This ensures compliance without automation.
  • Build an extension that listens to the production order release event. The extension can call the batch determination logic (via API or service) and update the order’s component list with assigned batches. This mimics value 4 behavior but outside the core system, keeping Clean Core intact.
  • But please note, SAP recommends performing batch determination at goods issue for components. This ensures that the actual available batches are considered at the point of consumption, avoiding mismatches between planned and actual inventory. It is the standard approach in Public Cloud.

entry.title: Re: Guidance Needed: SAP Learning Path for Procurement &...
entry.content:
Hello @uday_0-14 ,

I think a good starting point is Exploring SAP Cloud ERP. This is relevant for both public cloud and private cloud.

 

The decision on whether you should focus on Public Edition, Private Edition, or both is a very personal one and probably depends on where you see your career in the next few years (consulting, in-house, key user, …) and also which system(s) your employer will work with.

For me personally, I see public cloud as the future and that’s where I focus my efforts on 😊

 

After completing above-mentioned entry point, my advice would be to start with the more general Implementing SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition and get comfortable here before focusing Implementing SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, Sourcing and Procurement.

Alternatively, for Private Edition you may start with Implementing SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, Sourcing and Procurement.

 

For each Learning Journey, SAP provides some information about the recommended prerequisites, like here:

 

Helpful links for you:

  • Exploring SAP Cloud ERP
  • Cloud Onboarding for SAP Cloud ERP
  • Getting Started with Support from SAP (Support Accreditation)
  • Implementing SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
  • Get Certified: Implementation Consultant - SAP S/4HANA Cloud Publ
  • Implementing SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, Sourcing and Procu
  • Get Certified: Implementation Consultant - SAP S/4HANA Cloud Publ
  • Implementing SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, Sourcing and Proc
  • Get Certified: SAP Certified - SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition,

 

For each Learning Journey that starts with "Implementing..." (links above), you can scroll to the very bottom to find:

  • Access to a practice system including a PDF with training exercises
  • Live Sessions in which topics from the learning journey are explained in more detail
  • Links to planned "Get Certified" Sessions, which will cover all content relevant for a Certification

 

I hope this helps.

Best regards & good luck on your journey,

Kerisha

entry.title: Best Practices for JWT Validation and JWKS Caching in SAP API Management using KVM
entry.content:
Hi everyone,
I’m designing a JWT validation flow in SAP API Management for an integration project. The goal is to keep the proxy environment‑agnostic while ensuring high performance through JWKS caching. I’d appreciate feedback on the approach and any potential improvements.

Current Flow Summary:

  1. Validate the request body to ensure it is well‑formed JSON.
  2. Remove sensitive headers (e.g., Cookies) before further processing.
  3. Read the IdP base URL from an environment‑specific KVM.
  4. Normalize the URL using JavaScript for service callouts.
  5. Look up JWKS in cache.
  6. Call the IdP’s .well-known/jwks.json endpoint if the cache is empty.
  7. Cache the JWKS (TTL: 120 seconds).
  8. Verify JWT (RS256, scopes).
  9. Validate the issuer claim against the KVM value.

    Are there alternative or recommended ways to further externalize configuration (beyond KVMs) while keeping the proxy fully environment‑agnostic?

    Looking forward to your insights on optimizing this zero‑touch setup.

    Best regards,
    Somya

entry.title: Re: Guidance Needed: SAP Learning Path for Procurement &...
entry.content:
Beginner Level

  • S4H00 – SAP S/4HANA Overview: Introductory course for learners new to SAP.
  • S4500 – Business Processes in SAP S/4HANA Sourcing and Procurement: Comprehensive overview of procurement processes.


Intermediate Level

  • S4PR1 – Procurement in SAP S/4HANA: Covers purchase requisitions, purchase orders, contracts, and supplier management.
  • S4PR2 – Advanced Procurement in SAP S/4HANA: Focuses on configuration, flexible workflows, and integration with other modules.
  • Hands-on labs: Practice configuring enterprise structures, source lists, and info records.


Advanced Level

  • Specialized courses on Inventory Management, Consumption-Based Planning, and Analytics.
  • S4H400 – Embedded Analytics Foundation: Learn how to use real-time reporting in procurement.
  • Project-based training: Configure end-to-end procure-to-pay (P2P) scenarios.


Certification Path

Target C_TS452 – SAP Certified Application Associate – SAP S/4HANA Sourcing and Procurement.

Prep with:

  • SAP Learning Hub subscription (official practice exams, e-learning).
  • Udemy/Edoxi courses aligned with C_TS452 exam blueprint.
  • Practice tests and case-based scenarios

Below would be a 12‑month structured roadmap you can follow to go from beginner → job‑ready → certified in SAP S/4HANA Sourcing & Procurement. It balances theory, practice, and certification prep so you steadily build confidence.

entry.title: Re: Error while using SAP Fiori in browser
entry.content:
Hello，

It could be time out issue

I suggest you to try modifying the parameters controlling timeout:

fiori launchpad: http/security_session_timeout kba 2735916 Fiori Launchpad session timeout is not working after setting parameter rdisp/plugin_auto_logout

https://me.sap.com/notes/2735916

webgui: rdisp/gui_auto_logout note 3329632 Timeout for ITS/WebGUI sessions

https://me.sap.com/notes/3329632

I also notice that the host in url is "fioriqa.adds.nwnatural.com". Can you confirm whether you are using web dispatcher?

If yes, please also modify the timeout parameter for web dispatcher:

1757252 How to analyse problems related to session loss, logoff or blank screens caused by timeout issues
https://me.sap.com/notes/1757252

Also the following SAP Note was released at Kernel Patch :

3639412 - Backend error after textfield input https://me.sap.com/notes/0003639412
To resolve the issue, kindly update your Kernel to latest version.

2221289 - How to install package sapwebgui https://me.sap.com/notes/2221289
3628821 - FAQ on Patching SAP Kernel https://me.sap.com/notes/3628821

After you implement SAP Note 3639412, please make sure to also implement the SAP Notes below:

3667332 - Long text issue after upgrade SAP kernel https://me.sap.com/notes/3667332
3674227 - Backend error after textedit input with popup https://me.sap.com/notes/3667332

Best regards,
Joe

entry.title: SAP SRM On-Premise Transition: Context, Timelines, Successors, and What to Do Next
entry.content:
SAP Supplier Relationship Management (SRM) is part of SAP Business Suite 7 and has long been the backbone for strategic and operational procurement—covering sourcing, supplier collaboration, requisitioning, purchasing, and contracts—integrated with SAP ERP (ECC) and, more recently, S/4HANA back ends. Many organizations adopted SRM to standardize processes, enforce compliance, and drive savings through centralization and automation. 

 SAP is shifting focus from SAP SRM to align with its strategic move to cloud-based offerings and the S/4HANA platform. In 2020, SAP announced mainstream maintenance for Business Suite 7 through the end of 2027, with optional extended maintenance through the end of 2030. SAP SRM follows this policy, guiding customers toward modern successors that deliver faster innovation, better user experiences, embedded analytics, and networked supplier collaboration. See SAP Note 2881788 and SAP’s “SAP Extends SAP S/4HANA Maintenance Through 2040” announcement for context. 

Supported SAP SRM releases from Q1 2026: 

SAP will provide mainstream maintenance for the following SAP Supplier Relationship Management (SRM) Enhancement Packages until December 31, 2027 and provide optional extended maintenance from January 1, 2028 until December 31, 2030: 

- EHP2 for SAP SRM 7.0 (release level 7.02): support non-Unicode and Unicode deployments. 

- EHP3 for SAP SRM 7.0 (release level 7.13): support non-Unicode and Unicode deployments. 

- EHP4 for SAP SRM 7.0 (release level 7.14): support Unicode deployments.  

Note: If you run non-Unicode on EHP2/EHP3, consider Unicode migration as part of risk mitigation and transition planning.

Maintenance timeline for SAP SRM and what each phase means: 

SAP will provide mainstream maintenance for the latest three EHPs only i.e. EHP4, EHP3 and EHP2 only. Maintenance timeline for SAP Business Suite 7 which also includes SAP SRM are as below. 

- Until December 31, 2027: Mainstream maintenance with full standard support for supported SAP SRM releases. 

- From January 1, 2028 (default): Customer-specific maintenance with limited support (no new innovations, constrained legal/regulatory coverage); SAP does not support customer-developed extensions in this phase. 

- Optional January 1, 2028 to December 31, 2030: Extended maintenance for an additional fee; applies to the entire SAP Business Suite 7 core applications landscape and related add-ons (not selectable for subsets). 

- From January 1, 2031: Only customer-specific maintenance is available.

Please be aware, extended maintenance is not available for a subset of the customer installation only. It always applies to the full landscape of SAP Business Suite 7 core applications and all respective SAP Business Suite 7 Add-Ons. 

After its mainstream maintenance periods end in 2027 SAP SRM will no longer receive full standard support from SAP, though it will enter a limited "customer-specific maintenance" phase. 

Please check out SAP Note 52505 for general details regarding the maintenance phases of SAP applications and specifically customer-specific maintenance. 

Successor options: 

SAP S/4HANA for Central Procurement: Centralizes operational procurement across ECC and/or S/4HANA with native ERP integration and embedded analytics—ideal for harmonizing processes and supporting a broader S/4HANA journey. 

SAP Ariba: Cloud-based suite for strategic sourcing, supplier management, contracts, guided buying, and multi-enterprise collaboration—suited to organizations prioritizing rapid cloud innovation and supplier network scale. 

Hybrid: Pair S/4HANA Central Procurement for operational buying with SAP Ariba for strategic sourcing and collaboration to balance risk and accelerate time-to-value. 

Public sector: SAP Ariba Sourcing and Procurement for Public Sector in S/4HANA (SAP Ariba PPS) is the recommended path for SRM Government Procurement (see SAP Note 3668214). 

SAP is transitioning away from SRM in alignment with its move to S/4HANA and cloud solutions. Mainstream support concludes in 2027, with optional extended maintenance available until 2030 and customer-specific maintenance thereafter. With this timeline in mind, now is the right moment to set a clear transition roadmap. 

 Choose the successor architecture that best fits your goals—S/4HANA for Central Procurement, SAP Ariba, or a hybrid approach (and Ariba PPS for public sector)—and plan a phased migration that accounts for your current release, Unicode posture, integrations, and regulatory needs. Taking these steps early will reduce risk, maintain compliance, and position procurement for modern, innovation-rich capabilities. For official guidance, consult SAP Note 2881788, SAP Note 3668214, and SAP’s S/4HANA maintenance announcement. Incase you have any further queries, please reach out to us at sap_srm_customer_care@sap.com

entry.title: Re: Invoice and item numbers on settlement document flexi...
entry.content: Yes, you need to use the Transfer Manager. The relevant Transfer Group is the one tied to Sales Volume / Billing Data (commonly the group used in rebate/settlement scenarios). Add invoice number (VBRK-VBELN) and line item fields (VBRP-POSNR) to that transfer group, then adjust the flexible overview to display them.

entry.title: In the interface for creating a purchase order, is there a profitability field under account assignm
entry.content:
Due to analytical requirements, we need the accounting documents generated after purchase order creation to be associated with dimensions such as profit center, project, and customer. For this purpose, we configured the profitability field under the account assignment tab of the purchase ord er in the system, and after testing with a goods receipt, the resulting accounting document did contain the data we needed. However, the interface documentation does not include the profitability field in the purchase order interface. May I ask whether the standard interface for purchase orders simply does not have a profitability field?

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/bb9f1469daf04bd894ab2167f8132a1a/c89eec80ec2043d980cb7b8c89e0a00a.html?locale=zh-CN&q=%E5%8F%91%E7%A5%A8API
